summary: This Mutual Termination Agreement ends the Domain Name License Agreement that Beijing SINA and Shanghai SINA Leju had signed on May 8, 2008. By signing, both companies agree that, as of the execution date, the original contract is completely terminated and none of its terms continue to apply. The agreement is the entire understanding between the parties and can only be changed in writing signed by both sides. It is governed by Chinese law, can be signed in multiple copies, and binds each party and their successors. The main risk is that once signed, the original licensing rights and obligations cease, so any reliance on those rights after the termination date could lead to disputes or loss of benefits.
termination_clause: 1. Beijing SINA and SINA Leju agree that, upon the date of execution of this Termination Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect, and, for the avoidance of doubt, no provisions of the Original Agreement survive such termination.

TouchStar grants the reseller the right to market, distribute, and license the software, while retaining the ability to work with other customers and appoint additional distributors. The reseller must follow all legal requirements, obtain TouchStar’s written approval before using any third‑party "Other Resellers," and ensure those parties comply with the agreement. The reseller is financially responsible for any unauthorized or illegal use of the software by its sub‑resellers, including reverse‑engineering or violations of export controls. summary: This Sponsorship Agreement sets out a relationship where Domini Social Investments LLC (the Sponsor) provides oversight, administrative and management services to the Domini Advisor Trust (the Trust). The Sponsor must maintain office facilities, supervise trustees, handle compliance filings, prepare meeting materials, and answer public inquiries, while the Trust pays all operating expenses and a fee of 0.50% of its average daily net assets to the Sponsor. The Sponsor’s liability is limited except for willful misconduct, bad faith, or gross negligence. Either party can end the agreement with at least 60 days’ written notice and no penalty. Risks include the Sponsor’s limited liability and the Trust’s responsibility for all expenses, which could be substantial.

summary: This Distributor Agreement lets WatchGuard Technologies (WGT) appoint European Micro as a non‑exclusive reseller of its hardware‑software product package in a defined territory. WGT supplies the products at discounted prices and provides training and support; the distributor must meet guaranteed minimum purchase levels, keep inventory, market the products, provide reseller support, and report sales data. Either party can end the agreement for cause—such as breach of obligations, insolvency, or unauthorized assignments—or, after the initial term, with notice and a termination fee equal to 50% of any unpaid guaranteed purchases. Risks include the distributor’s liability for failing to meet purchase minimums (triggering liquidated damages) and the loss of rights to market and use WGT’s trademarks upon termination.

summary: This Maintenance and Support Agreement sets out the relationship between EuroTel (the customer) and Sicap Ltd (the service provider) for the SICAP® PPB, VCA, and OTA software modules. Sicap Ltd must provide a standard hotline, up to 20 hours of telephone support per month, remote assistance, and change‑management services, while EuroTel is responsible for first‑level fault tracing, training its staff, and supplying necessary logs and system access. EuroTel may request additional remote or on‑site support, which will be billed separately. Risks include extra charges for misuse, third‑party software bugs, and any support outside normal working hours. If Sicap Ltd cannot resolve an emergency within the stipulated time, it must dispatch staff on the next available flight or train, and EuroTel’s personnel must be on‑site to follow instructions.
gold_liability: Sicap Ltd's liability for indirect loss, including consequential loss,       loss of profit, lost savings and loss caused by interruption of operations       is excluded.

summary: This Gas Franchise Agreement lets the Town of Vinton give Roanoke Gas Company the right to build, operate, and maintain a gas distribution system in the town for 20 years starting Jan. 1, 2016. In return, the gas company must pay a franchise fee that is a share of a $98,196 base fee, calculated each year from its gas sales in Vinton and two neighboring cities, and the fee must be paid by March 31 of the following year. The company also must keep a staffed business office in Vinton at least 40 hours a week, follow nondiscrimination rules, and send any required notices by certified mail or courier. Failure to pay the fee on time, keep the office open, or comply with the nondiscrimination requirement could constitute a breach and expose the company to penalties or loss of the franchise.

This Manufacturing and Supply Agreement sets out the relationship between Pfizer (the Manufacturer) and Upjohn (the Customer) after their corporate separation. Pfizer agrees to produce and deliver pharmaceutical products to Upjohn for sale in the defined territory, while Upjohn commits to purchase those products, provide forecasts, and pay the agreed prices. Both parties must follow strict manufacturing, quality, environmental, and regulatory standards, maintain records, and protect each other's confidential information. Risks include potential termination for cause, breach of anti‑bribery rules, or insolvency, which could disrupt supply and trigger penalties. Liability is limited by the agreement’s indemnification and limitation provisions, meaning each side’s exposure to damages is capped and subject to insurance. Non‑compliance with quality or regulatory requirements could lead to product rejection, disposal costs, or legal liability.

summary: This Consulting Agreement sets out the relationship between Immunotolerance, Inc. and consultant Alan Crane. Crane will provide advisory services and, in return, receive a large block of company stock and reimbursement for approved expenses, but no employee benefits. The Company can end the agreement at any time for cause—such as repeated failure to perform, dishonesty, or criminal conviction—and must pay for services rendered up to termination; Crane can quit with 30 days' notice. Crane must keep all proprietary and confidential information secret, return all company materials, and assign any inventions related to the company's business to the Company. Risks include loss of the consulting role if cause is found, strict non‑disclosure obligations, and the assignment of potentially valuable inventions to the Company.

summary: This agreement creates a five‑year, renewable partnership between T&B Seminars (owner of Tarek El Moussa’s name, image and related intellectual property) and Legacy Education Alliance (LEA) to develop and run a branded real‑estate education business. T&B grants LEA an exclusive worldwide license to use its intellectual property to create educational materials and run seminars, while LEA handles marketing, event planning, sales, and delivery of the products. Both parties must keep each other's confidential information private and share any improvements to the IP. T&B can terminate early if LEA fails to meet sales benchmarks, and either side can end the contract with 90‑day notice at the end of each five‑year term. Risks include loss of the license if sales targets aren’t met and potential disputes over ownership of jointly created work product.

summary: This endorsement updates an existing variable life insurance contract after Thrivent Life Insurance Company was dissolved and its assets transferred to Thrivent Financial for Lutherans.
